blockExoticSubdeps.description: Block dependencies whose source is not a registry or workspace.

detectPublisherChange.description: Emit a [`Reason::PublisherChange`] when the resolved version was published by a different npm account than the immediately-prior version. Default severity is `block`; downgrade via the `severity` map (e.g. `publisher-change: warn`).

detectVersionSurfaceChange.description: Emit a [`Reason::VersionSurfaceChange`] when the resolved version adds new `bin` entries or new lifecycle-script names compared to the immediately-prior released version. Off by default — legitimate releases regularly add CLIs — but extremely useful when scoped to direct deps via `direct.detectVersionSurfaceChange: true`.

flagDeprecated.description: Emit a [`Reason::DeprecatedVersion`] when the registry reports the resolved version as deprecated. Default severity is `block`; many projects will want `severity.deprecated-version: warn` during a clean-up rollout.

minimumReleaseAge.description: Minimum release age in minutes. `0` disables the check.

detectPublisherChange.description: Per-direct-dep override for [`Defaults::detect_publisher_change`]. When `Some(_)`, takes precedence for direct deps.

Severity.description: Severity assigned to a `Reason` by the policy. `Allow` suppresses the reason entirely; `Warn` records it but does not fail; `Block` fails.

description: Policy DSL for InstallGuard. See DESIGN.md §4 for full semantics.

severity.description: Per-reason severity overrides. Keys are kebab-case reason codes (e.g. `release-age-below-threshold`); values are `allow` (suppress), `warn`, or `block`. Reasons not listed keep their default severity (currently always `block`, preserving v1 semantics).

title: InstallGuard policy
